subject: Project update
snippet:
Hey Eric,

Just wanted to check in on where things stand with the data pipeline ...
body:
Hey Eric,

Just wanted to check in on where things stand with the data pipeline migration. We're heading into the final sprint and I want to make sure we're aligned before the Thursday sync with the broader team.

Can you send over a quick status on the ingestion layer? Specifically whether the schema validation errors from last week got resolved.

Tyler

subject: Re: Project update
snippet:
Hey Tyler,

Good timing. The schema validation issues are about 90% resolved — I...
body:
Hey Tyler,

Good timing. The schema validation issues are about 90% resolved — I pushed a fix for the nested object handling yesterday and it's been running clean in staging since then. I'm still tracking one edge case with null timestamps but that shouldn't be a blocker for Thursday.

I'll put together a short summary doc today and share it before EOD so you have it ahead of the sync. Let me know if there's anything specific you want called out.

Eric

subject: Team meeting Thursday
snippet:
Hi Eric,

I'm pulling together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting ...
body:
Hi Eric,

I'm pulling together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting and wanted to get your input. I'm planning to cover the fall semester shift schedule, the new onboarding checklist for incoming student workers, and a quick debrief on the registration week network spike. Does that cover what you think needs to be discussed?

Also, are you okay to present the registration week incident summary? You wrote up the most complete timeline.

Tyler

subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday
snippet:
Hey Tyler,

The agenda looks solid. I'd maybe add a short item on the printer queue...
body:
Hey Tyler,

The agenda looks solid. I'd maybe add a short item on the printer queue situation in Building 7 — it's been coming up in Slack a lot and it might be easier to just address everyone at once rather than fielding individual questions all week.

And yes, I can walk through the incident summary. I'll put together three or four slides so there's something to anchor the discussion. Should I send them to you first or just share directly with the team?

Eric

subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday
snippet:
Good call on the printer queue — I'll add it to the agenda.

Just share the slides...
body:
Good call on the printer queue — I'll add it to the agenda.

Just share the slides directly with the team before the meeting, no need to loop me in first. I'll send the full agenda out tomorrow morning so everyone has time to prep.

Tyler

subject: Financial Aid Disbursement Notification — Fall 2025
snippet:
Dear Eric Lambert,

Your fall 2025 financial aid disbursement has been processed...
body:
Dear Eric Lambert,

Your fall 2025 financial aid disbursement has been processed and approved. A transfer of $2,800.00 will be deposited to your account on file within 3-5 business days.

If you have questions about your award package, please contact the Financial Aid office at finaid@lakeviewu.edu or visit us in Connelly Hall, Room 104.

Lakeview University Financial Aid Office

subject: Re: Financial Aid Disbursement Notification — Fall 2025
snippet:
Hi,

Thank you for the notification. I had expected $3,200 based on my original ...
body:
Hi,

Thank you for the notification. I had expected $3,200 based on my original award letter from August, but the disbursement shows $2,800. Could you help me understand the $400 difference before the deposit processes?

Thank you,
Eric Lambert

subject: Re: Financial Aid Disbursement Notification — Fall 2025
snippet:
Hi Eric,

The $400 adjustment reflects the outside scholarship you reported...
body:
Hi Eric,

The $400 adjustment reflects the outside scholarship you reported on your FAFSA. Per federal packaging guidelines, the outside scholarship reduced your need-based institutional grant by an equivalent amount, resulting in the $2,800 disbursement.

If you believe this is an error, you may request a formal review through our office. Please bring your original award letter and scholarship documentation.

Best,
Lakeview Financial Aid Office

subject: Re: Financial Aid Disbursement Notification — Fall 2025
snippet: Got it, that makes sense. Thanks for explaining — I wasn't aware the outside...
body:
Got it, that makes sense. Thanks for explaining — I wasn't aware the outside scholarship would reduce the need-based grant. I'll keep that in mind for next year's FAFSA. No review needed.

Eric Lambert

subject: FERPA Training — Required Before October 31
snippet:
Hi Eric,

All student workers in the IT department are required to complete...
body:
Hi Eric,

All student workers in the IT department are required to complete the annual FERPA compliance training before October 31st. This is a university-wide requirement and affects anyone with access to student information systems.

You can access the training module through the HR portal under Learning & Development. Should take about 45 minutes. Let me know if you have any trouble logging in.

Dana

subject: Re: FERPA Training — Required Before October 31
snippet:
Hi Dana,

Thanks for the heads up. Is this the module through the Cornerstone...
body:
Hi Dana,

Thanks for the heads up. Is this the module through the Cornerstone portal or the new HR system? I want to make sure I'm logging into the right place.

Eric

subject: Re: FERPA Training — Required Before October 31
snippet: It's the Cornerstone portal — same one you used for the onboarding modules...
body:
It's the Cornerstone portal — same one you used for the onboarding modules last fall. Log in with your lakeviewu credentials and search for FERPA Annual Compliance. The quiz at the end requires 80% to pass. The section on student record access is where most people get tripped up, so pay attention there.

Dana

subject: Re: FERPA Training — Required Before October 31
snippet: Got it, I'll knock it out Thursday when the desk is slower. Thanks for the tip...
body:
Got it, I'll knock it out Thursday when the desk is slower. Thanks for the tip on the student record access section — I'll make sure to take notes on that part.

Eric

subject: Re: FERPA Training — Required Before October 31
snippet: Perfect. Let me know when you've completed it — I track completions for the...
body:
Perfect. Let me know when you've completed it — I track completions for the department to make sure we're all covered before the deadline. Just a quick reply when you're done is fine.

Dana

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet:
Hey,

I'm free Thursday afternoon and could use a reason to get out for a bit...
body:
Hey,

I'm free Thursday afternoon and could use a reason to get out for a bit. Any chance you're free for lunch around 12:30 or 1?

Mateo

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday works perfectly. I've been wanting to try that Thai place on Clark...
body:
Thursday works perfectly. I've been wanting to try that Thai place on Clark — are you up for that? I can leave the desk around 12:30 and meet you there.

Eric

subject: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet:
Hey,

I have a gap in my morning before a 10am thing next Tuesday...
body:
Hey,

I have a gap in my morning before a 10am thing next Tuesday and thought it might be nice to sit down somewhere that isn't either of our apartments for once. Are you free for coffee around 8:30 or 9?

Mateo

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: 9am works great — I'm open until 11. That new place near the Loyola library...
body:
9am works great — I'm open until 11. That new place near the Loyola library has been on my list to try for a while if you want to go somewhere different. Or we can do our usual spot if you'd rather not risk it on a Tuesday morning.

Eric

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Let's try the library place — I looked it up and the reviews are solid. 9am Tues...
body:
Let's try the library place — I looked it up and the reviews are solid. 9am Tuesday, I'll meet you there. If it's a disappointment at least we'll know for next time.

Mateo

subject: Birthday brunch plans
snippet:
Hey,

I've been meaning to nail down Priya's birthday brunch — are we still doin...
body:
Hey,

I've been meaning to nail down Priya's birthday brunch — are we still doing the Sunday before her actual birthday? That would be the 16th. I want to get the reservation in soon before it fills up on a weekend morning.

Mateo

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Yes, the 16th works for me. I was thinking 10:30 or 11am so it doesn't drag too...
body:
Yes, the 16th works for me. I was thinking 10:30 or 11am so it doesn't drag too far into the afternoon. Do you have a final headcount yet? Last I heard it was somewhere around six or seven people.

Eric

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: It's seven now — Jess just confirmed she can make it. I'll book for eight to...
body:
It's seven now — Jess just confirmed she can make it. I'll book for eight to have a little buffer. Did you want to try somewhere new or stick with the place on Broadway? I know Priya likes it there but I'm open either way.

Mateo

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Let's do Broadway — it's reliable and it's her birthday, so no need to experiment...
body:
Let's do Broadway — it's reliable and it's her birthday, so no need to experiment. Go ahead and book it and I'll Venmo you for half the deposit whenever you're ready.

Eric

subject: ComEd bill
snippet: hey the ComEd bill just came — $84.20 for October. should I pay it...
body: hey the ComEd bill just came — $84.20 for October. should I pay it and you Venmo me half or do you want to set up separate accounts? also do you know how to read the usage chart because it looks like we used way more power in the first two weeks

subject: Re: ComEd bill
snippet: I'll Venmo you $42.10 now. The usage spike in the first two weeks...
body:
I'll Venmo you $42.10 now. The usage spike in the first two weeks was probably the window AC — I kept mine on until like Oct 12 because the apartment was still warm. Once we switched it off I think usage dropped. Can you forward me the account number? I want to set up autopay so we stop getting late notices.

Eric

subject: Re: ComEd bill
snippet: sent! and yeah that tracks — I finally turned the living room AC off two weeks ago...
body: sent! and yeah that tracks — I finally turned the living room AC off two weeks ago so the November bill should be way lower. also don't forget we owe Jess $37 each for the wifi router she bought last month — she asked me today if we could settle that soon

subject: Re: ComEd bill
snippet: Yeah I'll Venmo Jess today. Tell her sorry for being slow on it...
body:
Yeah I'll Venmo Jess today. Tell her sorry for being slow on it — I kept forgetting. And the Venmo to you is sent, let me know if it goes through.

Eric

subject: Re: ComEd bill
snippet: Got it, Venmo received! I'll tell Jess you sent hers too...
body: Got it, Venmo received! I'll tell Jess you sent hers too. She said she wants to come over Friday if you're around — apparently she found a new show she wants us all to watch and she's very serious about this

subject: Your Amazon order has been confirmed
snippet:
Hello Eric Lambert,

Your order has been confirmed. Introduction to Algorithms...
body:
Hello Eric Lambert,

Your order has been confirmed.

Order #112-4837291-0283745
Item: Introduction to Algorithms (CLRS) — 4th Edition — $89.95
Shipping: Standard (Free)
Estimated delivery: November 12–15, 2025
Shipping to: 7214 N. Clark Street, Apt 3F, Chicago, IL 60626

Thank you for shopping with Amazon.

subject: Fwd: Your Amazon order has been confirmed
snippet: Hey — this is the CLRS algorithms book. If you end up taking CS301 next semester...
body:
Hey — this is the CLRS algorithms book. If you end up taking CS301 next semester this is the main text and used copies don't really exist in good condition. Figured I'd forward it since you mentioned you might take that course. I'll leave it on the kitchen table when it arrives so you can flip through it.

Eric

subject: Account Alert: Deposit Received
snippet: A deposit of $2,800.00 has been credited to your Lakeview University...
body:
A deposit of $2,800.00 has been credited to your Lakeview University Financial Aid Disbursement account ending in 3377 on November 20, 2025.

If you did not authorize this transaction, please contact us immediately.

Chase Bank

subject: Financial aid came in!
snippet: Hey mom, financial aid disbursement hit today — $2,800. That means rent is...
body:
Hey mom, financial aid disbursement hit today — $2,800. That means rent is covered through the end of the semester without touching my IT paychecks, which is a huge relief. I'm going to set aside $200 for textbooks and keep the rest as a cushion.

Also I know October was tight over there — can I send you guys $100? I want to.

Eric

subject: Re: Financial aid came in!
snippet: Mi amor I'm so proud of you. Don't send the $100 — put it toward your books...
body:
Mi amor I'm so proud of you. Don't send the $100 — put it toward your books or keep it saved. We're fine over here. Your father and I are just happy you're doing so well.

Call me Sunday? I'll make your favorite soup.

Te quiero, mami

subject: Lakeview CS Club Fall Hackathon — Registration Open
snippet: The Lakeview CS Club Fall Hackathon is now open for registration...
body:
The Lakeview CS Club Fall Hackathon is now open for registration!

Date: November 15–16, 2025
Theme: Civic Technology
Team size: 2–4 members
Prizes: $500 (1st), $250 (2nd), $100 (3rd) + sponsor swag
Deadline to register: November 10, 2025

Register at csclub.lakeviewu.edu/hackathon — teams or individuals welcome.

See you there,
Lakeview CS Club

subject: CS Club hackathon — want to team up?
snippet: Priya — you want to do the CS Club hackathon? Civic tech theme...
body:
Priya — you want to do the CS Club hackathon? Civic tech theme, I was thinking we could build something around Chicago transit delay data — like a real-time delay tracker with a simple prediction component. You're better at frontend than I am so this division of labor would actually work.

Registration closes Nov 10.

Eric

subject: Re: CS Club hackathon — want to team up?
snippet: yes!! I've been wanting to do a hackathon. Jess might want to join too...
body:
yes!! I've been wanting to do a hackathon. Jess might want to join too — should we ask her? Three people is actually better coverage, and she knows some city open data APIs from her urban systems class.

Priya

subject: Re: CS Club hackathon — want to team up?
snippet: ask her. if she's in we need a project idea locked before Nov 10...
body:
ask her. if she's in we need a project idea locked before Nov 10. I'm still thinking Chicago transit — the CTA publishes real-time GTFS data and it's free. Simple enough to ship something functional in 24 hours if we're disciplined about scope. No feature creep this time.

Eric

subject: CS101 tutoring — can we schedule this week?
snippet:
Hi Eric,

I'm struggling with recursive functions and my midterm is next Tuesday...
body:
Hi Eric,

I'm really struggling with recursive functions and my midterm is next Tuesday. Would it be possible to schedule a tutoring session this week? I'm available Wednesday and Thursday evenings after 5pm.

Thank you,
Julia Martinez

subject: Re: CS101 tutoring — can we schedule this week?
snippet:
Hi Julia,

Wednesday at 6pm works well. We can focus on recursion — base cases...
body:
Hi Julia,

Wednesday at 6pm works well. We can focus on recursion — specifically base cases and how to trace through the call stack on paper. I find drawing it out manually really helps it click.

Meet me in the Connelly Hall study room 214. Bring your most recent assignment so we can start from something that actually confused you.

Eric

subject: Re: CS101 tutoring — can we schedule this week?
snippet: Perfect! Should I bring just my textbook or also the Canvas problem sets?
body:
Perfect, thank you! Should I bring just my textbook or also the Canvas problem sets from this week?

Julia

subject: Re: CS101 tutoring — can we schedule this week?
snippet: Bring both. The Canvas problem sets are better practice...
body:
Bring both. The Canvas problem sets are actually better practice than the textbook exercises for this topic. Also pull up your most recent assignment or any recursion problem you got wrong on a quiz — that's usually the most useful starting point.

See you Wednesday.

Eric

subject: Re: CS101 tutoring — can we schedule this week?
snippet: Got it! I'll have everything ready. See you Wednesday at 6 :)
body:
Got it! I'll have everything ready. See you Wednesday at 6 :)

Julia

subject: Application Received — Software Engineering Intern, Prism Financial Inc.
snippet: Thank you for applying to Software Engineering Intern at Prism Financial Inc...
body:
Thank you for applying to Software Engineering Intern at Prism Financial Inc.

We have received your application and will review it within 2–3 weeks. You will hear from us via this email address if we'd like to move forward.

Application ID: GH-20251210-04827

Greenhouse Recruiting

subject: Reference request — SWE internship application
snippet:
Hi Professor Kim,

I hope you're doing well. I submitted my first SWE internship...
body:
Hi Professor Kim,

I hope you're doing well. I submitted my first SWE internship application today — Prism Financial Inc., a Chicago fintech company — and listed you as a reference. I wanted to give you a heads up in case they reach out.

I'd be happy to share my resume and the job description if that would be helpful as context. And thank you again for your continued support — your data structures course is a big part of why I felt ready to apply.

Best,
Eric Lambert

subject: Weekend plans?
snippet: hey what are you thinking for this weekend? I want to leave the apartment...
body: hey what are you thinking for this weekend? I've been studying too much and my brain is turning into mush. I want to actually go somewhere on Saturday that isn't a coffee shop or the library.

subject: Re: Weekend plans?
snippet: Ha, fair. There's a street food thing in Andersonville on Saturday...
body:
Ha, fair. There's a street food thing in Andersonville on Saturday afternoon — I saw it in the Rogers Park subreddit. And I've been meaning to check out that thrift store in Wicker Park that Jess keeps mentioning. Could do both if you're up for it. Saturday food, Sunday thrifting.

Eric

subject: Re: Weekend plans?
snippet: Both of those sound perfect. Let's do the festival Saturday around noon...
body:
Both of those sound perfect. Let's do the food thing Saturday around noon and I'll ask Jess about Sunday thrifting — she'll definitely want to come. This weekend is officially a plan.

Priya
